creator: emz@norma.perm.ru
text:
FreeBSD: FreeBSD san2 11.0-BETA3 FreeBSD 11.0-BETA3 #1 r303697M: Wed Aug  3 11:31:52 MSK 2016     emz@san2:/usr/obj/usr/src/sys/SAN  amd64

net-snmp (from ports): 

Sypnosis:
=========

I said "nonsense" and I meant two things:
- snmpd thinks that the host is only receiving traffic, and not sending, that's not true.
- snmpd thinks that there's a HUGE amount of errors, that's not true either. One can suppose that this counter does correspond to some error counter in the netstat, but I don't see any that would fit.

Plus, I have an identical server with identical ix(4) interface, same net-snmp version (and same config) and it's output counters aren't zero (or almost close to zero). It's built with same port configuration/options, so I have no idea what triggers this weird behaviour.

[root@san2:/etc]# snmpd -v

NET-SNMP version:  5.7.3
Web:               http://www.net-snmp.org/
Email:             net-snmp-coders@lists.sourceforge.net

[root@san2:/etc]# pkg info net-snmp
net-snmp-5.7.3_11
Name           : net-snmp
Version        : 5.7.3_11
Installed on   : Sat Jul  9 07:38:43 2016 MSK
[...]

Snmpwalking:
